Now, next tone out here, this one's a good one called the pre-supposing tone. It's when you use a tone or voice that makes it sound like it's so good that it's even beyond question whether or not they're going to make a decision or whether or not they'll make money with something. Let's say it's an investment you're selling. So, and you're looking to sort of, you know, you're close to getting them there, but they're not quite there. And you say, listen, you know, listen, start off, pick up, you know, you know, start off with, you know, program 101. You know, you'll make money with it. But more importantly, what we could do for you over the long term in the way of this and that, and you start listing other benefits. But that first part, you say, you'll make money with it. And you actually use a tone that's so powerful what I have that it's beyond even whether or not it's good or bad. We all know it's good. And then they're like, yeah, they start thinking, well, they say, yeah, I guess I'll make them, they almost say you're right. They almost like sign up. Yeah, I guess he's right because you use the tone out. It simply presupposes that they're going to make the decision. It presupposes that something is good. And it's a very powerful way to get someone to kind of move that last 5% from saying, yeah, it sounds pretty good to say, yeah, yeah, it's good beyond, beyond approach, not even a point talking about. It's called your presupposing tone.
